prompt:
Our espresso-machine fleet streams brew telemetry into a reporting job, and the analytics folks keep hand-rolling loops for the same three aggregations. I want them as REAL java.util.stream.Collector implementations — built with Collector.of, honest characteristics, working combiners — because these run inside parallel pipelines and the last hand-rolled one silently lost data when the stream was split. No libraries; two sibling .java files next to the tests (no packages, source-launcher style).

BrewStats.java: public record BrewStats(long count, long sum, long min, long max). Plain value type over some long-valued metric.

BrewCollectors.java: public final class BrewCollectors with three static factories. All argument validation happens IN THE FACTORY (before any stream runs), throwing IllegalArgumentException.

1) public static <T> Collector<T, ?, List<T>> topK(int k, Comparator<? super T> order)
   The k GREATEST elements per `order`, greatest first. Ties are broken by encounter order — of two order-equal elements, the earlier one ranks first, and the combiner must preserve that when parts are merged (everything in the left part precedes the right part). k == 0 collects an empty list; k bigger than the input returns everything sorted; k < 0 or a null comparator is rejected at the factory. The finished list is immutable. This collector is order-sensitive and has a real finisher, so it must claim NO characteristics at all — an UNORDERED or IDENTITY_FINISH claim here would be a lie the parallel framework acts on.

2) public static <T> Collector<T, ?, Map<Boolean, BrewStats>> partitionStats(Predicate<? super T> match, ToLongFunction<? super T> value)
   Like partitioningBy but finishing straight into per-partition BrewStats over value(t): count, sum, min, max. The finished map is unmodifiable and ALWAYS contains exactly the keys TRUE and FALSE; an empty partition is BrewStats(0, 0, 0, 0). Careful with the combiner: merging an empty partial into a non-empty one must not drag min/max toward the zero sentinel. Characteristics: exactly UNORDERED (result is order-insensitive; the finisher is real, so no IDENTITY_FINISH).

3) public static <T> Collector<T, ?, NavigableMap<Long, Long>> histogram(ToLongFunction<? super T> value, long bucketWidth)
   Counts values into fixed-width buckets keyed by the bucket's lower bound. The bucket for v is the greatest multiple of bucketWidth that is <= v — that must hold for NEGATIVE values too (a plain truncating division rounds the wrong way there; -250 at width 100 belongs to bucket -300, not -200). Only non-empty buckets appear; keys ascend (it's a NavigableMap); the finished map is unmodifiable. bucketWidth must be positive and the value function non-null, enforced at the factory. Characteristics: exactly UNORDERED.

The suite in TestMain.java is the contract — it probes the characteristics sets directly, drives supplier/accumulator/combiner/finisher by hand over pre-split parts (including an empty part), reruns the collectors on reversed input and in parallel streams, and pins the tie-breaking. Don't modify it. Done = `java TestMain.java` all PASS, exit 0.
